rulings.Case Title: PAUL GREENFIELD VS. SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 25, 2023 line 1. RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT, LAWRENCE GOLDZBAND DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is continued to September 21, 2023 for the parties to comply with SFRL 2.7B and deliver courtesy copies of their respective filings in support of and in opposition to the motion with cover letters reflecting the new hearing date no later than August 31, 2023. (Courtesy copy of the moving papers received). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 644 BROADWAY, LLC VS. CHINA LIVE VENTURES LIMITED, L.P.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 25, 2023 line 10. DEFENDANT CHINA LIVE VENTURES LIMITED, L.P. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to Exhibit 6 and references thereto. Under the SF Commercial Eviction Moratorium, the rent while accruing, did not become due until the expiration of the forbearance period, which was within one year of the service of the notice. Motion is GRANTED as to the paragraph 19(i). Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that contractual damages (other than rent) are recoverable in a summary unlawful detainer provision. The Court notes that Plaintiff appears to seek BOTH the holdover damages at the rate of $5,400 (paragraph 19(g)) and the damages per paragraph 19.1 of the Lease. Plaintiff fails to explain how both are recoverable for the same time period. Plaintiff shall have 5 day leave to amend to allege that Lease 19.1 provision is the measure of damages Plaintiff seeks to recover under 19(g) (as opposed to other available measures, e.g. contract rent, FMV, etc). If Plaintiff fails to amend within 5 days, Defendant shall file an answer within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Plaintiff to prepare an order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
